commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Therefore the LORD was very angry with Israel, and removed them out of his sight: there was none left but the tribe of Judah only. Sight, as objects of horror, cast away from the temple, and from the promised land. Tribe, or kingdom. See 3 Kings xii. Israel began to be rejected by God, when the schism took place. (Haydock) It was entirely lost, when Salmanasar took the people into captivity. Some few were left; and these formed a part of the kingdom of Josias, on their returning to the service of the true God, (2 Paralipomenon xxxiv. ) while others fled into Egypt, Osee viii.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
